sections.paragraph:
Βηθλέεμ, ἡ (other edd.-λεέμ; s. B-D-F §56, 3) indecl. (בֵּית לֶחֶם house of bread; LXX; TestReub 3:13; AscIs; Just. In Joseph. Βήθλεμα, Βηθλέεμα [Ant. 5, 323],-ων [5, 136] and Βηθλ[ε]έμη, ης [7, 312]) Bethlehem, a town in Judaea, 7 km. south of Jerusalem, birthplace of Benjamin (Gen. 1:16; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 10 Jac. εἰς Ἐφραθά, ἣν εἶναι B.) and home of David (1 Km 17:12; 20:6=Jos., Ant. 6, 227), hence the birthplace of the Messiah: Mt 2:1, 5f (Mi 5:1), 8, 16; Lk 2:4, 15; J 7:42; GJs 17:1; 18:1; 21:1, 2 (v.l.). Cp. Baedeker7 1910, 94ff; TZahn, D. Geburtsstätte in Gesch., Sage u. bildender Kunst: NKZ 32, 1921, 669ff; Dalman, Orte3 18–60 [Eng. tr. 17–55]; WFoerster, ZDPV 57, ’34, 1–7; CKopp, Holy Places of the Gospels, ’63, 1–47; BHHW I 233; OEANE I 302.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. km. = kilometer

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Baedeker Baedeker = KB., Palästina u. Syrien—List 6

NKZ NKZ = Neue Kirchliche Zeitschrift—List 6

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

Eng. tr. Eng. tr. = English translation

ZDPV ZDPV = Zeitschrift der Deutschen Palästina-Vereins—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

OEANE OEANE = Oxford Encyclopedia of Archaeology in the Near East—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 174
